review.reason: Bounded skill optimization is directly relevant to loop-engineered improvement, but claims, license, and execution surfaces need review.
review.action: Review license, bounded edit method, held-out validation, rejected-edit buffer, rollback evidence, and whether a safe doctrine summary should be queued.
review.result: Reviewed as MIT-licensed reference-only source profile. Created normalizer follow-up for validation-gated skill-improvement doctrine. No upstream content, code, benchmark, transcript, package, or CLI surface ingested.

review.reason: Large public skill corpus may expose reusable metadata/corpus patterns or skip-note evidence, but provenance and safety are unknown.
review.action: Review license, provenance, prompt-injection/execution risk, hidden instructions, redundancy, and whether source-profile value exists.
review.result: Reviewed as MIT-licensed reference-only source profile. Created normalizer follow-up for shared skill-library governance: canonical skill pool, named collections, runtime-specific AGENTS.md versus tool/CLI exposure, registry persistence, collision handling, and execution/install/upload risk separation. No upstream content, code, examples, templates, package, or CLI surface ingested.

review.reason: Known upstream source mattpocock/skills has a recently visible teach skill and changelog activity. Since prior Matt Pocock skills were already distilled, new or materially changed skills must be reviewed as deltas instead of missed by broad discovery.
review.action: Review license, changelog, teach skill boundary, asset/component reuse pattern, invocation surface, overlap with existing Architectonic skills, and whether to create a source profile, skip/watch note, risk item, or original normalization queue. Do not copy upstream prose, templates, lesson examples, or assets.
review.result: Reviewed as MIT-licensed reference-only source profile. Created normalizer follow-up for mission-grounded learning workspace doctrine: learner mission, durable learning records, short lesson artifacts, reference artifacts, reusable lesson components, cited-current-source requirement, wisdom/community boundary, and explicit workspace write boundary. No upstream prose, templates, examples, assets, component files, or skill bodies were copied.

normalization.action: Create an original Architectonic playbook for mission-grounded learning workspaces without copying upstream prose, format files, lesson examples, asset/component files, command documentation, or skill bodies.

catalog.reason: A new skill file was added; catalog and install manifest surfaces must be rebuilt or verified before publication/package endorsement.
catalog.action: Run or request catalog refresh for dist/catalog.json, dist/catalog.md, and dist/install-manifest.json; verify skill count and review/risk metadata after rebuild.
catalog.result: Created dist/skills/validation-gated-skill-improvement/SKILL.md, verified dist/catalog.json and dist/catalog.md at 1174 skills, 97 agent-operations entries, and 410 medium-risk entries, and verified dist/install-manifest.json still points installers to the rebuilt catalog surfaces.

catalog.reason: The connector pass safely repaired package visibility and human catalog counts, but the machine-readable catalog is too large for safe manual replacement without running the catalog builder.
catalog.action: Run npm run build:catalog in a local checkout or CI-capable surface, then verify dist/catalog.json, dist/catalog.md, and dist/install-manifest.json agree on the validation-gated skill entry and counts.
catalog.result: Catalog Refresh rebuilt dist/catalog.json to 1174 entries. The machine catalog now matches dist/catalog.md summary counts and preserves install-manifest discovery pointers.

catalog.reason: A workflow-watched dist skill path changed to trigger catalog rebuild, but the scheduler cannot observe or wait for the resulting workflow commit in the same connector pass.
catalog.action: Verify whether the Catalog Refresh workflow regenerated dist/catalog.json, dist/catalog.md, and dist/install-manifest.json. If not, run npm run build:catalog in a checkout or CI-capable surface and commit the generated surfaces.
catalog.result: Verified rebuilt distribution catalog surfaces directly on main. No manual catalog JSON replacement was required.
